behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: This failure appears at the module boundary between prompt/request construction and the API client call. A unit test of a helper can miss dropped/omitted labels during wiring or serialization; a local integration test with a capturing fake API client catches that real handoff failure without needing real network.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier with a fake in-process API client that records outgoing payloads. Call classify() with a sample document, then assert the sent request contains every taxonomy label exactly once (or in the required format) before returning.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: The core failure is local validation/mapping logic: checking API output against the allowed taxonomy and substituting "unknown". This can be caught without real I/O by invoking the response-handling logic directly.
behaviors.test_description: Call the label-normalization/validation path with an out-of-taxonomy label (for example, "medical") and assert the returned value is exactly "unknown". Include a valid label control case to ensure only invalid values are remapped.

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model behavior (non-determinism) from the external AI API. Mocked responses cannot reveal drift/variance across repeated real calls, which is the actual failure mode this behavior targets.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated real API classifications for the same fixed document (for example, 30-100 trials under production settings) and assert label consistency, or assert consistency after any intended stabilization mechanism (such as cache key reuse) is applied end-to-end.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is real API token accounting and request/response usage behavior. Mock token counters can hide real over-budget usage caused by provider tokenizer differences or actual request packaging.
behaviors.test_description: Execute classification against the real API with representative documents and read provider-reported token usage for each call. Assert total tokens per call are <= configured budget, and fail if any exceedance occurs.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is retry-policy orchestration across components (API client error mapping, backoff/wait, retry loop). A pure unit test is often too narrow and can miss wiring issues; real external API is not required if a controllable fake can emit rate-limit responses.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client that returns a rate-limit error (for example, 429 or equivalent) on first call and success on second. Verify classify() does not fail immediately, performs at least one delayed retry (using fake clock or measurable delay hook), and ultimately returns the successful label.
